html:
Exclusive to:  

The Moose/Goose Egg is an object in the Reign of Giants DLC. During Spring, Moose/Goose will wander off to lay an egg. The egg radiates electricity and can be struck with a Hammer 4 times to cause it to hatch, otherwise if the Moose/Goose is left alone, it will hatch on its own. Each strike with the Hammer deals 10 damage to the player. A Weather Pain can be used to hatch the egg from a distance without taking damage. 5 Moslings will hatch from the egg and attack if the player gets too close.

Once the Egg hatches, the small nest will remain behind, giving Moose/Goose the possibility to lay another Egg. At the end of Spring, both the egg and the nest will despawn.

Trivia

  • The egg will randomly alternate between being called a "Moose Egg" and a "Goose Egg".

Bugs

  • Occasionally, the Moose/Goose will spawn, and no egg will spawn, even if left alone for days.
  • Spawning in this item spawns an empty nest. To fix this, you need to add the 'InitEgg()' variable to the end. Eg: 'c_spawn("mooseegg"):InitEgg()'
  • Occasionally a Moose/Goose egg that did not hatch while the Moose/Goose was still alive will remain in place, unhatched through all the seasons. Using wormholes, moving many screens away or relogging will not cause the egg to hatch or despawn.
  • Dying by hammering the egg will result in the morgue listing the death as "Shenanigans".

Gallery

  • Wilson next to an egg for scale.
  • The egg radiating electricity.
  • An empty nest.
  • A set piece found in Don't Starve Together where a Moose/Goose nest will be spawned during spring.
  • A Moose/Goose egg, as seen in the Spring teaser trailer for Reign of Giants.
  • A Moose/Goose egg struck by lightning, as seen in the Spring teaser trailer for Reign of Giants.
  • A Moose/Goose egg found in Don't Starve: Pocket Edition (Android).
DSTDSRoGSWHam

"mooseegg"
“Its contents are like excited electrons trying to escape.”
–Wilson
“It's huuuuge!”
–Willow
“Breakfast for whole family!”
–Wolfgang
“Not sure how a thing like that happens.”
–Wendy
“PROBES UNABLE TO PENETRATE EXTERIOR SHELL”
–WX-78
“What a marvel!”
–Wickerbottom
“Well, it lays eggs, apparently.”
–Woodie
“Ride the lightning, you big egg.”
–Maxwell
“Giving off a fair amount of heat.”
–Wagstaff
“Sömething is böuncing aröund inside.”
–Wigfrid
“That would make a huge breakfast!”
–Webber
“It will one day hatch into a moo... a goo... a thing.”
–Walani
“I think I'll leave this egg quite alone!”
–Warly
“Whut beast lurks wit'in!”
–Woodlegs
“'TIS BIG NUM NUMS”
–Wilba
“Tweeter inside”
–Wormwood
“A tempest in an egg cup.”
–Wheeler
“Animals don't build things well.”
–Winona
“The egg of that most fearsome creature.”
–Wortox
“Could crack open and see what inside?”
–Wurt
“I can't wait for them to hatch!”
–Walter
“A brand new lifetime getting ready to start.”
–Wanda
The Moose/Goose Egg is an object in the Reign of Giants DLC. During Spring, Moose/Goose will wander off to lay an egg. The egg radiates electricity and can be struck with a Hammer 4 times to cause it to hatch, otherwise if the Moose/Goose is left alone, it will hatch on its own. Each strike with the Hammer deals 10 damage to the player. A Weather Pain can be used to hatch the egg from a distance without taking damage. 5 Moslings will hatch from the egg and attack if the player gets too close.
Once the Egg hatches, the small nest will remain behind, giving Moose/Goose the possibility to lay another Egg. At the end of Spring, both the egg and the nest will despawn.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The egg will randomly alternate between being called a "Moose Egg" and a "Goose Egg".
Bugs[edit | edit source]
Occasionally, the Moose/Goose will spawn, and no egg will spawn, even if left alone for days.
Spawning in this item spawns an empty nest. To fix this, you need to add the 'InitEgg()' variable to the end. Eg: 'c_spawn("mooseegg"):InitEgg()'
Occasionally a Moose/Goose egg that did not hatch while the Moose/Goose was still alive will remain in place, unhatched through all the seasons. Using wormholes, moving many screens away or relogging will not cause the egg to hatch or despawn.
Dying by hammering the egg will result in the morgue listing the death as "Shenanigans".

The '''Moose/Goose Egg''' is an object in the ''[[Reign of Giants]]'' DLC. During [[Spring]], [[Moose/Goose]] will wander off to lay an egg. The egg radiates electricity and can be struck with a [[Hammer]] 4 times to cause it to hatch, otherwise if the Moose/Goose is left alone, it will hatch on its own. Each strike with the Hammer deals 10 damage to the player. A [[Weather Pain]] can be used to hatch the egg from a distance without taking damage. 5 [[Mosling]]s will hatch from the egg and attack if the player gets too close.

Once the Egg hatches, the small nest will remain behind, giving Moose/Goose the possibility to lay another Egg. At the end of Spring, both the egg and the nest will despawn.
__NOTOC__
=={{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* The egg will randomly alternate between being called a "Moose Egg" and a "Goose Egg".

== {{Pic32|Mosquito}} Bugs ==
* Occasionally, the Moose/Goose will spawn, and no egg will spawn, even if left alone for days.
* Spawning in this item spawns an empty nest. To fix this, you need to add the 'InitEgg()' variable to the end. Eg: 'c_spawn("mooseegg"):InitEgg()'
* Occasionally a Moose/Goose egg that did not hatch while the Moose/Goose was still alive will remain in place, unhatched through all the seasons. Using wormholes, moving many screens away or relogging will not cause the egg to hatch or despawn.
* Dying by hammering the egg will result in the morgue listing the death as "Shenanigans".
